test_cases.description: RegisterOrgHandlers mounts all ten organization routes on the Echo group and each route is reachable.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
An Echo instance is created with APIGroup() returning a *echo.Group
test_cases.input: Call RegisterOrgHandlers(g, db) where g is a valid *echo.Group and db is a valid *sql.DB
test_cases.expected: The Echo router recognises POST /orgs, GET /orgs, GET /orgs/:id, PATCH /orgs/:id, DELETE /orgs/:id, POST /orgs/:id/block, POST /orgs/:id/unblock, GET /orgs/:id/members, PUT /orgs/:id/members/:user_id, DELETE /orgs/:id/members/:user_id; each route returns a non-405 status when called with a valid authenticated request

test_cases.description: Every org endpoint response carries the Cache-Control: no-store header inherited from the APIGroup middleware.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
RegisterOrgHandlers called with a valid group and db
An admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: The HTTP response includes a Cache-Control header with value 'no-store'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httptest_GET("/orgs", admin_token)
assert resp.Header.Get("Cache-Control") == "no-store"

test_cases.description: A valid POST /orgs request from an admin creates the organization and returns HTTP 201 with a correct OrgResponse.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Admin credential is available
No organization named 'Acme Corp' or slug 'acme-corp' exists
test_cases.input: POST /orgs with JSON body {"name": "Acme Corp", "slug": "acme-corp", "url": "https://acme.example.com"} and admin auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response body contains id (valid UUID v4), name='Acme Corp', slug='acme-corp', url='https://acme.example.com', status='active', created_at equals updated_at, both are RFC 3339 UTC timestamps
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := POST("/orgs", body={name:"Acme Corp", slug:"acme-corp", url:"https://acme.example.com"}, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 201
body := parse_json(resp)
assert is_valid_uuid_v4(body.id)
assert body.name == "Acme Corp"
assert body.slug == "acme-corp"
assert body.url == "https://acme.example.com"
assert body.status == "active"
assert body.created_at == body.updated_at
assert is_rfc3339_utc(body.created_at)

test_cases.description: POST /orgs with a missing or whitespace-only name returns HTTP 400 with 'name is required'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: POST /orgs with JSON body {"name": "   ", "slug": "acme-corp"} and admin auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body is {"error": {"code": 400, "message": "name is required"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := POST("/orgs", body={name:"   ", slug:"acme-corp"}, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 400
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.code == 400
assert body.error.message == "name is required"

test_cases.description: POST /orgs with a missing slug returns HTTP 400 with 'slug is required'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: POST /orgs with JSON body {"name": "Acme Corp"} (slug omitted) and admin auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error message 'slug is required'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := POST("/orgs", body={name:"Acme Corp"}, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 400
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "slug is required"

test_cases.description: POST /orgs with a slug containing invalid characters returns HTTP 400 with 'invalid slug format'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: POST /orgs with JSON body {"name": "Acme Corp", "slug": "Acme Corp!"} and admin auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error message 'invalid slug format'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := POST("/orgs", body={name:"Acme Corp", slug:"Acme Corp!"}, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 400
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "invalid slug format"

An organization with name 'Existing Corp' and slug 'acme-corp' already exists in the orgs table

test_cases.description: POST /orgs from a non-admin user returns HTTP 403 with 'forbidden'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
A non-admin user credential is available
test_cases.input: POST /orgs with JSON body {"name": "Acme Corp", "slug": "acme-corp"} and non-admin auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; response body contains error message 'forbidden'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:

assert resp.StatusCode == 403
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "forbidden"

test_cases.description: GET /orgs without include_blocked returns only active organizations ordered by name ascending.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Three organizations exist: 'Zebra Corp' (active), 'Alpha Corp' (active), 'Beta Corp' (blocked)
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: GET /orgs with admin auth header and no query parameters
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is a JSON array containing 'Alpha Corp' and 'Zebra Corp' in that order; 'Beta Corp' is absent
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json_array(resp)
assert len(body) == 2
assert body[0].name == "Alpha Corp"
assert body[1].name == "Zebra Corp"
assert none_of(body, lambda o: o.name == "Beta Corp")

test_cases.description: GET /orgs?include_blocked=true returns all organizations including blocked ones, ordered by name ascending.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Two organizations exist: 'Alpha Corp' (active), 'Beta Corp' (blocked)
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is a JSON array containing both 'Alpha Corp' and 'Beta Corp'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs?include_blocked=true", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json_array(resp)
names := map(body, lambda o: o.name)
assert "Alpha Corp" in names
assert "Beta Corp" in names

test_cases.description: GET /orgs returns an empty JSON array when no organizations exist.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with an empty orgs table
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is exactly []
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json_array(resp)
assert len(body) == 0

test_cases.description: GET /orgs from a non-admin user returns HTTP 403 with 'forbidden'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
A non-admin user credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; response body contains error message 'forbidden'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:

assert resp.StatusCode == 403
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "forbidden"

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id from an admin returns HTTP 200 with OrgResponse and an ETag header.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An organization with a known UUID exists in the orgs table
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is OrgResponse with the correct org fields; response includes ETag header
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.id == org_id
assert resp.Header.Get("ETag") != ""

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id from a non-admin org member returns HTTP 200 with the OrgResponse.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
An organization with a known UUID exists
A non-admin user exists and has a row in org_members for this org
Non-admin user credential is available
test_cases.input: GET /orgs/{org_id} with non-admin member auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is OrgResponse matching the org
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id, member_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.id == org_id

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id from a non-admin non-member user returns HTTP 403 with 'forbidden'.

body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "forbidden"

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id for a non-existent organization returns HTTP 404 with 'organization not found'.

Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: GET /orgs/{random_valid_uuid} with admin auth header where the UUID does not exist in orgs
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body contains error message 'organization not found'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id with an invalid UUID path parameter returns HTTP 400 with 'invalid organization id'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error message 'invalid organization id'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:

assert resp.StatusCode == 400
body := parse_json(resp)

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id with an If-None-Match header matching the current ETag returns HTTP 304 with no body.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An organization exists with a known updated_at timestamp
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: GET /orgs/{org_id} with admin auth header and If-None-Match header set to the current ETag value derived from updated_at
test_cases.expected: HTTP 304; response body is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
first_resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id, admin_token)
etag := first_resp.Header.Get("ETag")
resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id, admin_token, headers={"If-None-Match": etag})
assert resp.StatusCode == 304
assert len(resp.Body) == 0

test_cases.description: PATCH /orgs/:id with a valid name update returns HTTP 200 with updated OrgResponse and an incremented updated_at.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An organization with a known UUID exists with name 'Acme Corp'
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: PATCH /orgs/{org_id} with JSON body {"name": "Acme Corporation"} and admin auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body has name='Acme Corporation', slug unchanged, updated_at >= original updated_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
original := GET("/orgs/" + org_id, admin_token).body
resp := PATCH("/orgs/" + org_id, body={name:"Acme Corporation"}, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.name == "Acme Corporation"
assert body.slug == original.slug
assert parse_time(body.updated_at) >= parse_time(original.updated_at)

test_cases.description: PATCH /orgs/:id with a slug field in the body silently ignores it and keeps the original slug.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An organization with slug 'original-slug' exists
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: PATCH /orgs/{org_id} with JSON body {"name": "New Name", "slug": "new-slug"} and admin auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body has name='New Name' but slug remains 'original-slug'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := PATCH("/orgs/" + org_id, body={name:"New Name", slug:"new-slug"}, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.name == "New Name"
assert body.slug == "original-slug"

test_cases.description: PATCH /orgs/:id with a body containing no recognized fields returns HTTP 400 with 'no fields to update'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An organization with a known UUID exists
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: PATCH /orgs/{org_id} with JSON body {} and admin auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error message 'no fields to update'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:

assert resp.StatusCode == 400
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "no fields to update"

test_cases.description: DELETE /orgs/:id from an admin deletes the org, cascades member deletion, and returns HTTP 204.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
An organization with a known UUID exists with one member row in org_members
A user account exists in users
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204 with no body; the org row is absent from orgs; the org_members row is absent; the user row in users still exists
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := DELETE("/orgs/" + org_id, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 204
assert len(resp.Body) == 0
assert db_query_count("SELECT * FROM orgs WHERE id=?", org_id) == 0
assert db_query_count("SELECT * FROM org_members WHERE org_id=?", org_id) == 0
assert db_query_count("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=?", user_id) == 1

test_cases.description: DELETE /orgs/:id for a non-existent organization returns HTTP 404 with 'organization not found'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with an empty orgs table
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body contains error message 'organization not found'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := DELETE("/orgs/" + non_existent_uuid, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 404
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "organization not found"

test_cases.description: DELETE /orgs/:id with an invalid UUID path parameter returns HTTP 400 with 'invalid organization id'.

test_cases.description: DELETE /orgs/:id from a non-admin user returns HTTP 403 with 'forbidden'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An organization with a known UUID exists
A non-admin user credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; response body contains error message 'forbidden'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:

test_cases.description: POST /orgs/:id/block on an active org returns HTTP 200 with status='blocked' and an updated updated_at.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An active organization with a known UUID exists
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body has status='blocked'; updated_at is >= the original updated_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
original := GET("/orgs/" + org_id, admin_token).body
resp := POST("/orgs/" + org_id + "/block", nil, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.status == "blocked"
assert parse_time(body.updated_at) >= parse_time(original.updated_at)

test_cases.description: POST /orgs/:id/block on an already-blocked org is idempotent and returns HTTP 200 with the unchanged org.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An organization with status='blocked' exists with a known UUID
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: POST /orgs/{org_id}/block with admin auth header (org is already blocked)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body has status='blocked'; updated_at is the same as before the second call
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
first_resp := POST("/orgs/" + org_id + "/block", nil, admin_token)
first_body := parse_json(first_resp)
second_resp := POST("/orgs/" + org_id + "/block", nil, admin_token)
second_body := parse_json(second_resp)
assert second_resp.StatusCode == 200
assert second_body.status == "blocked"
assert second_body.updated_at == first_body.updated_at

test_cases.description: POST /orgs/:id/block for a non-existent organization returns HTTP 404 with 'organization not found'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with an empty orgs table
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body contains error message 'organization not found'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := POST("/orgs/" + non_existent_uuid + "/block", nil, admin_token)

body := parse_json(resp)

test_cases.description: POST /orgs/:id/unblock on a blocked org returns HTTP 200 with status='active' and an updated updated_at.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An organization with status='blocked' exists with a known UUID
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body has status='active'; updated_at is >= the blocked updated_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
blocked := GET("/orgs/" + org_id, admin_token).body
resp := POST("/orgs/" + org_id + "/unblock", nil, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.status == "active"
assert parse_time(body.updated_at) >= parse_time(blocked.updated_at)

test_cases.description: POST /orgs/:id/unblock on an already-active org is idempotent and returns HTTP 200 with the unchanged org.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
An active organization with a known UUID exists
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: POST /orgs/{org_id}/unblock with admin auth header (org is already active)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body has status='active'; updated_at is unchanged from before the call
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
original := GET("/orgs/" + org_id, admin_token).body
resp := POST("/orgs/" + org_id + "/unblock", nil, admin_token)
body := parse_json(resp)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
assert body.status == "active"
assert body.updated_at == original.updated_at

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id/members from an admin returns HTTP 200 with all member details ordered by username.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
An organization exists with two members: user 'bob' and user 'alice'
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is a JSON array of OrgMemberResponse objects ordered alphabetically by username; first element is 'alice', second is 'bob'; each contains user_id, username, email, role, joined_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json_array(resp)
assert len(body) == 2
assert body[0].username == "alice"
assert body[1].username == "bob"
for m in body:
  assert m.user_id != ""
  assert m.username != ""
  assert m.email != ""
  assert m.role in ["admin", "user"]
  assert is_rfc3339_utc(m.joined_at)

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id/members from a non-admin org member returns HTTP 200 with the member list.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
An organization exists with the requesting user as a member
Non-admin member credential is available
test_cases.input: GET /orgs/{org_id}/members with non-admin member auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is a JSON array of OrgMemberResponse objects
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members", member_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json_array(resp)
assert type(body) == list

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id/members from a non-admin non-member returns HTTP 403 with 'forbidden'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
An organization exists; the requesting user is not a member
Non-admin non-member credential is available
test_cases.input: GET /orgs/{org_id}/members with non-admin non-member auth header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; response body contains error message 'forbidden'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members", non_member_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 403
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "forbidden"

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id/members for a non-existent organization returns HTTP 404 with 'organization not found'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with an empty orgs table
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body contains error message 'organization not found'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs/" + non_existent_uuid + "/members", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 404
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "organization not found"

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id/members for an org with no members returns HTTP 200 with an empty array.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs and org_members tables
An organization exists with no rows in org_members
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is exactly []
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 200
body := parse_json_array(resp)
assert len(body) == 0

test_cases.description: GET /orgs/:id/members with an invalid UUID path parameter returns HTTP 400 with 'invalid organization id'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error message 'invalid organization id'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := GET("/orgs/not-a-uuid/members", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 400
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "invalid organization id"

test_cases.description: PUT /orgs/:id/members/:user_id from an admin adds the user to the org and returns HTTP 204.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
An organization and a user exist with known UUIDs; the user is not yet a member
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204 with no body; a row with (org_id, user_id) exists in org_members with a valid created_at timestamp
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := PUT("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members/" + user_id, nil, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 204
assert len(resp.Body) == 0
assert db_query_count("SELECT * FROM org_members WHERE org_id=? AND user_id=?", org_id, user_id) == 1

test_cases.description: PUT /orgs/:id/members/:user_id for an already-existing member is idempotent and returns HTTP 204.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
The user is already a member of the org (row exists in org_members)
Admin credential is available
test_cases.input: PUT /orgs/{org_id}/members/{user_id} with admin auth header (user already a member)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204 with no body; exactly one row exists in org_members for (org_id, user_id)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
first_resp := PUT("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members/" + user_id, nil, admin_token)
assert first_resp.StatusCode == 204
second_resp := PUT("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members/" + user_id, nil, admin_token)
assert second_resp.StatusCode == 204
assert db_query_count("SELECT * FROM org_members WHERE org_id=? AND user_id=?", org_id, user_id) == 1

test_cases.description: PUT /orgs/:id/members/:user_id with a non-existent org returns HTTP 404 with 'organization not found'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs and users tables

assert resp.StatusCode == 404
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "organization not found"

test_cases.description: DELETE /orgs/:id/members/:user_id from an admin removes the member and returns HTTP 204; user account is preserved.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs, org_members, and users tables
An organization exists with a member; the org_members row exists
Admin credential is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204 with no body; the org_members row is removed; the user row in users still exists
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := DELETE("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members/" + user_id, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 204
assert len(resp.Body) == 0
assert db_query_count("SELECT * FROM org_members WHERE org_id=? AND user_id=?", org_id, user_id) == 0
assert db_query_count("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=?", user_id) == 1

test_cases.description: DELETE /orgs/:id/members/:user_id where membership doesn't exist returns HTTP 404 with 'membership not found'.

body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "membership not found"

test_cases.description: A slug consisting of valid lowercase alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores passes slug validation.
preconditions: Slug validator function is accessible
test_cases.input: slug = 'my-valid_slug123' (length 16, all valid chars, no leading/trailing hyphen or underscore)
test_cases.expected: Validation passes with no error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
err := validateSlug("my-valid_slug123")
assert err == nil

test_cases.description: A slug containing uppercase letters is rejected with 'invalid slug format'.
preconditions: Slug validator function is accessible
test_cases.expected: Validation returns error 'invalid slug format'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
err := validateSlug("MySlug")
assert err != nil
assert err.message == "invalid slug format"

test_cases.description: A slug starting with a hyphen or underscore is rejected with 'invalid slug format'.
preconditions: Slug validator function is accessible
test_cases.expected: Validation returns error 'invalid slug format'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for slug in ["-bad-start", "_bad-start"]:
  err := validateSlug(slug)
  assert err != nil
  assert err.message == "invalid slug format"

test_cases.description: A slug ending with a hyphen or underscore is rejected with 'invalid slug format'.
preconditions: Slug validator function is accessible
test_cases.expected: Validation returns error 'invalid slug format'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for slug in ["bad-end-", "bad-end_"]:
  err := validateSlug(slug)
  assert err != nil
  assert err.message == "invalid slug format"

test_cases.description: A slug longer than 128 characters is rejected with 'invalid slug format'.
preconditions: Slug validator function is accessible
test_cases.expected: Validation returns error 'invalid slug format'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
slug := repeat('a', 129)
err := validateSlug(slug)
assert err != nil
assert err.message == "invalid slug format"

test_cases.description: isOrgMember returns (true, nil) when a matching row exists in org_members.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the org_members table
A row with (org_id='org-uuid', user_id='user-uuid') exists in org_members
test_cases.expected: Returns (true, nil)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err := isOrgMember(db, org_uuid, user_uuid)
assert err == nil
assert result == true

test_cases.description: isOrgMember returns (false, nil) when no matching row exists in org_members.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with an empty org_members table
test_cases.input: Call isOrgMember(db, 'org-uuid', 'user-uuid') where no such row exists
test_cases.expected: Returns (false, nil)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err := isOrgMember(db, org_uuid, user_uuid)
assert err == nil
assert result == false

test_cases.description: All org endpoints return HTTP 401 when accessed without a valid authentication credential.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised
RegisterOrgHandlers called with a valid Echo group and db
Auth middleware is attached to the APIGroup
test_cases.input: Send requests to POST /orgs, GET /orgs, GET /orgs/{uuid}, PATCH /orgs/{uuid}, DELETE /orgs/{uuid}, POST /orgs/{uuid}/block, POST /orgs/{uuid}/unblock, GET /orgs/{uuid}/members, PUT /orgs/{uuid}/members/{uuid}, DELETE /orgs/{uuid}/members/{uuid} — all without auth headers
test_cases.expected: All ten requests return HTTP 401
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
endpoints := [("POST","/orgs"), ("GET","/orgs"), ("GET","/orgs/"+uuid), ...]
for method, path in endpoints:
  resp := http_request(method, path, no_auth)
  assert resp.StatusCode == 401

property_tests.description: Slug immutability: for any organization, updateOrg, blockOrg, and unblockOrg never change the slug value set at creation.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an arbitrary valid org (name, slug, url). Apply any combination of updateOrg (with name/url changes), blockOrg, and unblockOrg operations.
property_tests.invariant_check: After each mutating operation, assert that org.slug equals the slug provided at creation time.

property_tests.description: updated_at monotonicity: after any mutating operation (update, block, unblock) on an org, the updated_at timestamp is >= the previous updated_at.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an arbitrary valid org. Apply a random sequence of updateOrg, blockOrg, unblockOrg operations, recording the updated_at value before and after each.
property_tests.invariant_check: For each operation in the sequence, assert parse_time(new_updated_at) >= parse_time(old_updated_at). The sequence is bounded to at most 20 operations.

property_tests.description: Blocked org listing exclusion: GET /orgs without include_blocked=true never returns orgs with status='blocked'.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an arbitrary set of organizations (mix of active and blocked). Call GET /orgs without include_blocked.
property_tests.invariant_check: Assert that none of the returned orgs have status='blocked'. Repeat for any subset of blocked organizations.

property_tests.description: Cascade delete completeness: after deleteOrg, no org_members rows with the deleted org's id remain; user rows are unaffected.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an org with an arbitrary number (0 to 10) of members. Delete the org via deleteOrg.
property_tests.invariant_check: After deletion, assert db_query_count('SELECT * FROM org_members WHERE org_id=?', org_id) == 0. Assert db_query_count('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id IN (?)', member_user_ids) == original_user_count.

property_tests.description: Idempotent block: calling blockOrg N times produces the same org state as calling it once; subsequent calls do not change updated_at beyond the first block.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an arbitrary active org. Call blockOrg N times (N randomly chosen from 1 to 5), recording the response after each call.
property_tests.invariant_check: All calls return HTTP 200 with status='blocked'. The updated_at from the second and subsequent calls equals the updated_at from the first block call. The loop always terminates after at most 5 iterations.

property_tests.description: Idempotent unblock: calling unblockOrg N times produces the same org state as calling it once; subsequent calls do not change updated_at.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an arbitrary blocked org. Call unblockOrg N times (N randomly chosen from 1 to 5), recording the response after each call.
property_tests.invariant_check: All calls return HTTP 200 with status='active'. The updated_at from the second and subsequent calls equals the updated_at from the first unblock call. The loop always terminates after at most 5 iterations.

property_tests.description: Idempotent add member: calling addOrgMember with the same (org_id, user_id) pair any number of times results in exactly one row in org_members and always returns HTTP 204.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate a valid org and user pair. Call addOrgMember with the same pair N times (N randomly chosen from 1 to 5).
property_tests.invariant_check: All N calls return HTTP 204. After all calls, db_query_count('SELECT * FROM org_members WHERE org_id=? AND user_id=?', org_id, user_id) == 1. Loop terminates after at most 5 iterations.

property_tests.description: Unique name and slug: no two distinct organizations share the same name, and no two share the same slug.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an arbitrary set of orgs with distinct names and slugs. Attempt to create a duplicate by reusing an existing name or slug.
property_tests.invariant_check: For any attempt to create an org with a name or slug matching an existing org, assert the response is HTTP 409. Query the orgs table and assert all name values are distinct and all slug values are distinct.

property_tests.description: No unauthenticated access: any request to any org endpoint without a valid credential returns HTTP 401 and no org data.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an arbitrary valid org endpoint (method + path) from the ten registered routes. Send the request without an authentication header.
property_tests.invariant_check: Assert resp.StatusCode == 401. Assert that no org fields (id, name, slug, status) appear in the response body.

property_tests.description: Cache-Control no-store on all org responses: every response from any org handler includes Cache-Control: no-store.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each of the ten org routes, send a valid authenticated request (as admin).
property_tests.invariant_check: Assert resp.Header.Get('Cache-Control') contains 'no-store' for every response.

edge_case_tests.description: RegisterOrgHandlers panics immediately when passed a nil *sql.DB before any routes are registered.
preconditions: An Echo instance is created with a valid *echo.Group
edge_case_tests.input: Call RegisterOrgHandlers(g, nil) where g is a valid *echo.Group and db is nil
edge_case_tests.expected: A panic is raised with a descriptive message; no routes are registered on the group

edge_case_tests.description: POST /orgs with a slug exactly 129 characters long returns HTTP 400 with 'invalid slug format'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Admin credential is available
edge_case_tests.input: POST /orgs with JSON body {"name": "Test Org", "slug": "a" * 129} and admin auth header
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error message 'invalid slug format'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
slug := repeat('a', 129)
resp := POST("/orgs", body={name:"Test Org", slug:slug}, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 400
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "invalid slug format"

edge_case_tests.description: POST /orgs with a slug starting with a hyphen returns HTTP 400 with 'invalid slug format'.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Admin credential is available
edge_case_tests.input: POST /orgs with JSON body {"name": "Test Org", "slug": "-invalid"} and admin auth header
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error message 'invalid slug format'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
for slug in ["-invalid", "invalid-", "_invalid", "invalid_"]:
  resp := POST("/orgs", body={name:"Test Org", slug:slug}, admin_token)
  assert resp.StatusCode == 400
  body := parse_json(resp)
  assert body.error.message == "invalid slug format"

edge_case_tests.description: POST /orgs that fails with a non-UNIQUE database error returns HTTP 500 with 'internal server error' without leaking raw SQL.
preconditions: A mock or instrumented *sql.DB that returns a generic database error (not UNIQUE constraint) on INSERT
Admin credential is available
edge_case_tests.input: POST /orgs with valid JSON body {"name": "Test Org", "slug": "test-org"} and admin auth header; DB INSERT returns a generic error
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500; response body contains error message 'internal server error'; no raw SQL error text in response body
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_db.on_insert_return(generic_db_error)
resp := POST("/orgs", body={name:"Test Org", slug:"test-org"}, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 500
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "internal server error"
assert "SQL" not in resp.raw_body
assert "sqlite" not in resp.raw_body.lower()

edge_case_tests.description: POST /orgs with a malformed JSON body returns HTTP 400 with a descriptive error.
preconditions: An in-memory SQLite database is initialised with the orgs table
Admin credential is available
edge_case_tests.input: POST /orgs with body 'not valid json' and Content-Type: application/json and admin auth header
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains an error message
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := POST_raw("/orgs", body="not valid json", content_type="application/json", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 400
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.code == 400
assert body.error.message != ""

edge_case_tests.description: GET /orgs/:id/members when the join query fails returns HTTP 500 with 'internal server error'.
preconditions: A mock or instrumented *sql.DB where org lookup succeeds but the org_members JOIN users query fails
Admin credential is available
edge_case_tests.input: GET /orgs/{org_id}/members with admin auth header; join query returns a generic error
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500; response body contains error message 'internal server error'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_db.on_member_join_query_return(generic_db_error)
resp := GET("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members", admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 500
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "internal server error"

edge_case_tests.description: PUT /orgs/:id/members/:user_id when the org_members INSERT fails with a non-PK-conflict error returns HTTP 500.
preconditions: A mock or instrumented *sql.DB where org and user lookups succeed but INSERT returns a generic error
Admin credential is available
edge_case_tests.input: PUT /orgs/{org_id}/members/{user_id} with admin auth header; INSERT fails with generic error
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500; response body contains error message 'internal server error'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_db.on_insert_return(generic_db_error)
resp := PUT("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members/" + user_id, nil, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 500
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "internal server error"

edge_case_tests.description: DELETE /orgs/:id/members/:user_id when the org_members DELETE fails returns HTTP 500 with 'internal server error'.
preconditions: A mock or instrumented *sql.DB where the DELETE on org_members returns a generic error
Admin credential is available
edge_case_tests.input: DELETE /orgs/{org_id}/members/{user_id} with admin auth header; DELETE returns a generic error
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500; response body contains error message 'internal server error'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_db.on_delete_return(generic_db_error)
resp := DELETE("/orgs/" + org_id + "/members/" + user_id, admin_token)
assert resp.StatusCode == 500
body := parse_json(resp)
assert body.error.message == "internal server error"

edge_case_tests.description: isOrgMember returns (false, error) when the org_members query fails with a database error.
preconditions: A mock or instrumented *sql.DB where the org_members query returns a generic error
edge_case_tests.input: Call isOrgMember(mock_db, 'org-uuid', 'user-uuid') where the query returns a generic database error
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns (false, non-nil error); does not panic
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_db.on_query_return(generic_db_error)
result, err := isOrgMember(mock_db, org_uuid, user_uuid)
assert result == false
assert err != nil

smoke_tests.description: Admin creates an organization end-to-end through the full request pipeline including auth middleware and database insertion.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /orgs with JSON body {"name": "Smoke Corp", "slug": "smoke-corp", "url": "https://smoke.example.com"} and a valid admin credential
expected_effects: HTTP 201 response with OrgResponse body
Response body id is a valid UUID v4
Response body status is 'active'
Response body created_at equals updated_at
A row exists in the orgs SQLite table with the given name and slug
Cache-Control: no-store header is present on the response

smoke_tests.description: An org member views their organization through the full request pipeline with the access control path (non-admin member allowed, non-member rejected).
smoke_tests.trigger: GET /orgs/{org_id} with a valid non-admin credential for a user who is a member of the org
expected_effects: HTTP 200 response with OrgResponse body matching the org
ETag response header is present and non-empty
A subsequent request with If-None-Match matching the ETag returns HTTP 304

smoke_tests.description: Admin blocks and unblocks an organization through the complete handler pipeline, verifying status transitions.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /orgs/{org_id}/block followed by POST /orgs/{org_id}/unblock with valid admin credentials
expected_effects: First POST /block returns HTTP 200 with status='blocked'
GET /orgs without include_blocked does not include the blocked org
Second POST /unblock returns HTTP 200 with status='active'
GET /orgs without include_blocked again includes the org

smoke_tests.description: Admin adds a member, lists members to verify appearance, then removes the member through the complete handler pipeline.
smoke_tests.trigger: PUT /orgs/{org_id}/members/{user_id}, then GET /orgs/{org_id}/members, then DELETE /orgs/{org_id}/members/{user_id} with valid admin credentials
expected_effects: PUT returns HTTP 204
GET returns HTTP 200 with an array containing the added user's details (user_id, username, email, role, joined_at)
DELETE returns HTTP 204
Subsequent GET returns HTTP 200 with an array that no longer contains the removed user

smoke_tests.description: Full organization lifecycle: create org, add member, update name, verify via GET, delete org, verify cascade and 404.
smoke_tests.trigger: Sequence: POST /orgs → PUT /orgs/{id}/members/{uid} → PATCH /orgs/{id} → GET /orgs/{id} → DELETE /orgs/{id} → GET /orgs/{id}
expected_effects: POST /orgs returns HTTP 201
PUT member returns HTTP 204
PATCH returns HTTP 200 with updated name and updated_at > created_at
GET after PATCH returns HTTP 200 with the new name
DELETE returns HTTP 204
GET after DELETE returns HTTP 404 with 'organization not found'
No rows remain in org_members for the deleted org_id
User rows in users table are unaffected

smoke_tests.description: Admin lists organizations with blocked filter: verify blocked org exclusion and inclusion via include_blocked param.
smoke_tests.trigger: GET /orgs → POST /orgs/{id}/block → GET /orgs → GET /orgs?include_blocked=true with valid admin credentials
expected_effects: Initial GET /orgs returns the org in the array
POST /block returns HTTP 200 with status='blocked'
GET /orgs (no param) returns HTTP 200 without the blocked org
GET /orgs?include_blocked=true returns HTTP 200 containing both active and blocked orgs
